pi_items.source: Task.json metadata matches the actual cancer drug used in the task description and verifier logic

FDA Prescribing Information and manufacturer data: Clarithromycin is a strong CYP3A4 inhibitor contraindicated during venetoclax ramp-up phase. Web search confirmed contraindication and major interaction status.

FDA Prescribing Information: Verapamil is a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor requiring 50% dose reduction. This is a clinically significant interaction but manageable (not contraindicated), corresponding to orange/amber severity in traffic light systems.

Pharmacokinetic analysis: Metformin is not a CYP3A4 inhibitor or inducer, nor a P-glycoprotein substrate/inhibitor. No documented pharmacokinetic interaction with venetoclax. Literature shows only synergistic anti-cancer effects, supporting GREEN classification.

Mechanism analysis: Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor with no known CYP3A4 or P-glycoprotein effects. No documented interaction with venetoclax in literature. GREEN classification is consistent with pharmacological mechanisms.

pi_items.verified_value: University of Liverpool and Radboudumc (Radboud University Medical Center)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed the app is developed collaboratively by these institutions. Wikipedia and Google Play Store verify the app's origin.

pi_items.verified_value: Red: Should not be coadministered; Orange/Amber: Significant interaction requiring monitoring/dose adjustment; Yellow: Weak interaction; Green: No significant interaction; Grey: Insufficient data
pi_items.source: Cancer-druginteractions.org archived documentation confirmed the Liverpool system uses 5 traffic light colors with specific clinical meanings.
Cancer-druginteractions.org archived site stated content was last updated in June 2022. Web service was taken offline September 2023.

privileged_info_summary: The Liverpool Cancer iChart is a real drug interaction database developed by the University of Liverpool and Radboudumc. The app uses a traffic light color system to classify drug interactions. The metadata claims for Venetoclax interactions are accurate: Clarithromycin is RED (strong CYP3A4 inhibitor, contraindicated), Verapamil is ORANGE (moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor requiring dose adjustment), Metformin is GREEN (no pharmacokinetic interaction documented), and Lisinopril is GREEN (no significant interaction expected). The interaction database is based on authoritative clinical sources and was last updated in June 2022. All claimed interaction colors align with FDA prescribing information and standard pharmacological mechanisms.
